records.ety: [OE. performen, parfourmen, parfournen, OF. parfornir, parfournir, to finish, complete; OF. & F. par (see Par) + fournir to finish, complete. The word has been influenced by form; cf. L. performare to form thoroughly. See Furnish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Performed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Performing.]

defs: To carry through; to bring to completion; to achieve; to accomplish; to execute; to do.
quotes: I will cry unto God most high, unto God that performeth all things for me.
Great force to perform what they did attempt.

defs: To discharge; to fulfill; to act up to; as, to perform a duty; to perform a promise or a vow.
To represent; to act; to play; as in drama.
quotes: Perform a part thou hast not done before.

defs: To do, execute, or accomplish something; to acquit one's self in any business; esp., to represent sometimes by action; to act a part; to play on a musical instrument; as, the players perform poorly; the musician performs on the organ.
